title: Fixing IndexError list index out of range in Synthesizer.generate_goldens_from_docs()

step_plan.summary: Fixes an IndexError in the docs→contexts→goldens synthesis pipeline by enforcing safe alignment between generated contexts and their source metadata, and by correcting an async index-shadowing bug that could corrupt source_file selection. Adds defensive indexing so goldens can still be produced even when ContextGenerator returns mismatched list lengths.

steps.operation: Normalize/validate the (contexts, source_files, context_scores) tuple returned from ContextGenerator before downstream golden generation; ensure lists are aligned or safely handled when lengths differ (e.g., pad source_files with None, truncate to min length, or record a per-context safe accessor).
steps.reason: The reported IndexError is triggered later when goldens are created by indexing source_files[i] while iterating contexts; the docs entrypoint is the earliest place to enforce the invariant that each context has a corresponding source_file (or None).
steps.side_effects: May change behavior when ContextGenerator yields mismatched lengths (e.g., some goldens will have source_file=None instead of raising). Could affect any callers relying on strict 1:1 mapping errors; ensure tests cover the new behavior.

steps.operation: Mirror the sync normalization logic in the async docs pipeline: after awaiting context generation, enforce safe alignment between contexts and source_files before invoking async golden generation; propagate context_scores consistently.
steps.reason: Async path should obey the same correctness invariants as sync; otherwise it can still pass mismatched lists into golden creation and trigger IndexError in async flows.
steps.side_effects: Async behavior may diverge from previous implicit assumptions; may require updating/adding async tests and ensuring any returned metadata (source_file/context_score) remains stable.

steps.operation: Harden golden creation against out-of-range indexing by guarding source_files and context_scores access per context (e.g., use a helper like `sf = source_files[i] if source_files and i < len(source_files) else None`; similarly for scores) and ensure the loop only assumes `contexts` length.
steps.reason: This is the direct surface where `source_files[i]` can raise IndexError when ContextGenerator returns fewer source_files than contexts (or when source_files is None). Defensive access guarantees no crash and preserves partial results.
steps.side_effects: Goldens may be created with missing source_file/score metadata in mismatch scenarios; downstream consumers of goldens should tolerate None. Might surface previously hidden upstream issues; consider logging/debug warnings if lengths differ.

steps.operation: Fix async index shadowing and unsafe indexing: preserve a dedicated `context_index` for selecting source_files[context_index]; rename the inner enumerate loop variable (e.g., `input_index`) to avoid overwriting, and apply the same bounds-checked access pattern for source_files/context_scores as in sync.
steps.reason: Reusing `index` for both the context position and per-input processing can corrupt which element is selected from source_files and can directly cause IndexError. Separating indices and guarding bounds ensures correct, stable source_file assignment per context in async generation.
steps.side_effects: Async goldens may now attach the correct source_file rather than an incorrect one; tests that implicitly relied on buggy ordering/mapping (unlikely) could change. Ensure concurrency/task creation logic remains unchanged aside from variable naming and safe lookups.
